title: Tomato Harvest Pricing Run Seed 42-2
scenario: Tomato Ketchup in Ontario. Supplier-to-manufacturer pricing sets the manufacturer cost basis before the retailer negotiation.

product_context.demand_signal: Retail ketchup demand is steady, but wholesale buyers want price protection before peak tomato procurement.
product_context.supply_signal: Tomato harvest yields are uneven across growing regions, putting pressure on paste input pricing.

agents.name: Supplier
agents.objective: Sell above the supplier reservation price while keeping volume committed.

agents.name: Manufacturer
agents.objective: Buy below the procurement ceiling and preserve downstream margin.

agents.name: Retailer
agents.objective: Buy enough units without exceeding the retail reservation price.

phases.name: supplier_manufacturer
phases.label: Supplier to Manufacturer
phases.description: The supplier negotiates an upstream unit price with the manufacturer.

phases.name: manufacturer_retailer
phases.label: Manufacturer to Retailer
phases.description: The manufacturer negotiates a downstream unit price with the retailer after the upstream deal sets its cost basis.

steps.message: Thanks for starting the discussion. Based on current market conditions, I can open at 6.70 per unit for the committed volume, which is in line with where supply is trading and gives you dependable coverage. If you have flexibility on volume or scheduling, I may be able to sharpen the pricing somewhat as we work through the details.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

tool_calls.result_summary: Reviewed state for supplier_manufacturer round 1. Pending message: none.
Observed market reference price: 6.38.

steps.message: I appreciate the opening, but 6.70 is well above where this program can work on our side. To get the conversation moving, I can put forward 2.30 per unit for the committed volume, with the understanding that firmer scheduling and clean release timing would help keep execution straightforward. If there's room to move materially from your opening, we can keep working toward something practical.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.30, below its hidden seller floor of 2.83.

delivered_message.note: Thanks for starting the discussion. Based on current market conditions, I can open at 6.70 per unit for the committed volume, which is in line with where supply is trading and gives you dependable coverage. If you have flexibility on volume or scheduling, I may be able to sharpen the pricing somewhat as we work through the details.

steps.message: Thanks for putting a number on the table. At 2.30, we're well below a workable level for this supply, especially against current market conditions, but I do want to keep the discussion moving. If you're prepared to commit the stated volume with firm scheduling and clean releases, I can move materially from my opening to 5.95 per unit as a serious next step.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

steps.message: I appreciate the movement, but 5.95 is still far outside a range that works for our finished-goods economics. We can improve from our 2.30 and put 2.44 per unit on the table if you can support the stated volume with reliable scheduling and clean releases. That reflects a real concession on our side to keep momentum, but we would need a substantially sharper number than your last offer to make this viable.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.44, below its hidden seller floor of 2.83.

steps.message: I appreciate the movement to 2.44, but that level is still well below a workable supply position for us given production and fulfillment commitments. To keep this moving, I can make a meaningful concession from 5.95 and come down to 5.20 per unit if the stated volume is firm and scheduling remains reliable. That narrows the gap substantially on our side and reflects real movement, but we would need pricing in this range to support the program responsibly.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

That is a substantial move from 5.20 on our side and reflects a serious effort to narrow this toward a workable close.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

steps.message: Thank you for the concession from 5.20 to 4.60; I recognize that as a real move. That said, 4.60 is still well outside the economics we can support on this program, especially with the margin we need to preserve downstream. To keep this moving in the limited time left, I can improve to 2.70 per unit if we can align on committed volume and clean execution. This is a meaningful step from our prior 2.50 and is intended as a serious attempt to narrow the gap toward something executable.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.70, below its hidden seller floor of 2.83.

negotiations.label: Supplier to Manufacturer
negotiations.outcome_summary: Supplier to Manufacturer reached the turn limit without agreement.

diagnosis.outcome: Upstream deal timeout. Downstream negotiation did not run because the upstream deal did not close.
diagnosis.chain_effect: The upstream deal did not establish a downstream cost basis, so phase two was skipped.
key_risks: A costly upstream agreement compresses downstream negotiating room.
Turn limits can force timeouts even when the gap is narrowing.
suggested_next_actions: Tune reservation prices and turn limits to test sensitivity.
Add richer quantity or margin tradeoffs before introducing LLM behavior.

notes: Backend simulation with linked upstream and downstream negotiations.
